title: Tibit MCMS / Juniper Unified PON — Key Findings
hypothesis: The VOLT OLT SFP is likely a Tibit MicroPlug OLT (or clone)

critical_discovery.title: Management VLAN 4090
critical_discovery.description: The PON Controller communicates with OLT devices on VLAN 4090 using a Linux VLAN subinterface (e.g., enp0s8.4090). This is the management plane VLAN.

protocol.name: IEEE 1904.2
protocol.full_name: Standard for Control and Management of EPON
protocol.quote: The MCMS PON Controller uses IEEE 1904.2 packets (L2) to communicate with the OLT and ONU devices.

layers.name: MCMS PON Manager
layers.description: Angular Web UI, Django REST API

mcms_stack_components.name: PON Manager
mcms_stack_components.description: Angular web app + Django REST API (Apache2, port 80/443)

mcms_stack_components.name: MongoDB
mcms_stack_components.description: Central datastore for config, state, stats, alarms

mcms_stack_components.name: Netconf Server
mcms_stack_components.description: YANG models (BBF TR-383, TR-385) via Netopeer2/Sysrepo

next_steps: Try VLAN 4090 instead of 4091 — this is the standard Tibit management VLAN
If VOLT OLT is truly a Tibit clone, it should respond on this VLAN
Consider deploying the actual Tibit MCMS PON Controller (it's a .deb package) to manage the OLT natively
